case_title: KIM KREIS et al VS. AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA INC. et al

docket_entries.description: ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KIM KREIS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS GRANTED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. COURT IMPOSES $200 SANCTIONS AGAINST BOTH COUNSEL TO BE PAID TO EACH OTHER FOR FAILURE TO MAKE REASONABLE GOOD FAITH EFFORTS TO CONFER. CCP 2023.020. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT OBJECTIONS THAT WERE TIMELY ASSERTED ARE PRESERVED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KIM KREIS'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY 12.3. THE REPRESENTATION IN THE OPPOSITION MEMORANDUM THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE NO RESPONSIVE INFORMATION SHOULD BE IN THE RESPONSE. GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1. DEFENDANTS NEED TO PROVIDE A SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSE AS TO EACH DEFENSE. GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 2. DEFENDANTS NEED TO PROVIDE TSANG'S EMPLOYER AND CONTACT INFORMATION. GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 10. DEFENDANTS NEED TO PROVIDE A SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSE AND/OR PREPARE A PRIVILEGE LOG. GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 21, 22, AND 23 AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS 9, 14, AND 15. THE DISCOVERY RELATES TO THE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANTS HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF A DEFECT ON THEIR PROPERTY. THE RESPONSES MAY BE LIMITED TO THE PAST 5 YEARS. IT IS NOT UNDULY BROAD OR BURDENSOME SINCE THE PARTIES AGREE THAT ONLY 39 FACILITIES ARE IMPLICATED. DENIED AS MOOT AS TO DOCUMENT REQUEST 19. DEFENDANTS REPRESENT THAT THE INSURANCE POLICIES WERE PROVIDED. ALL FURTHER RESPONSES DUE BY JANUARY 5, 2012. DEFENDANTS TO PAY $2,440 (8 HOURS AT $300 PER HOUR PLUS $40 IN COSTS) TO PLAINTIFF BY JANUARY 30, 2012. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Constance F. Morrison, Esq. of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP, Tel. 415.982.3600, for plaintiffs and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS KIM KREIS and KATRINA KREIS' Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Production Of Documents and For Monetary Sanctions is Granted in part. The parties agree that the motion regarding production of the IME report is moot since the report was recently produced. The motion to compel the PMK depositions noticed by Plaintiff is granted. The depositions shall occurr no later than September 19, 2012. The motion to compel production of Request Nos. 3 and 6 is granted. Defendant shall produce those documents at last one day prior to the commencement of the PMK depositions referenced above. Any documents withheld on privilege shall be identified on a log. All requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: JONATHAN LIEBEMAN, ESQ., ARCHER NORRIS, 925.830.6600, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT/CROSS DEFENDANT SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. cross complainant/cROSS DEFENDANT SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, or in the alternative, for summary adjudication is granted. Schindler satisfied its burden of production and AMC has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue that Schindler is liable for plaintiff's alleged injuries. AMC's request for a continuance is denied because it has not shown that evidence may exist to defeat the motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovry Adn Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KIM KREIS'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY AND MONETARY SANCTIONS.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to form interrogatory 12.3. The representation in the opposition memorandum that defendants have no responsive information should be in the response. Granted as to form interrogatory 15.1. Defendants need to provide a substantive response as to each defense. Granted as to special interrogatory 2. Defendants need to provide Tsang's employer and contact information. Granted as to special interrogatory 10. Defendants need to provide a substantive response and/or prepare a privilege log. Granted as to special interrogatories 21, 22, and 23 and document requests 9, 14, and 15. The discovery relates to the issue whether defendants had actual or constructive notice of a defect on their property. The responses may be limited to the past 5 years. It is not unduly broad or burdensome since the parties agree that only 39 facilities are implicated. Denied as moot as to document request 19. Defendants represent that the insurance policies were provided. All further responses due by January 5, 2012. Defendants to pay $2,440 (8 hours at $300 per hour plus $40 in costs) to plaintiff by January 30, 2012.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

Appearances: Constance F. Morrison of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP (982.3600) for plaintiff and moving party; Frank M. LaFleur of Manning & Kass Ellrod Ramirez Trester LLP (217.6990) for defendant and responding party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion to compel further response to interrogatories and request for production of documents granted. Request for sanctions denied. Court imposes $200 sanctions against both counsel to be paid to each other for failure to make reasonable good faith efforts to confer. CCP 2023.020. Further, the court indicated that objections that were timely asserted are preserved. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported
